name: Asparagus Soup #2
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Fresh asparagus washed and tough/dry ends removed
1 cup Carefully washed and chopped leeks white part only
1 small Baking potato (about 1/2 lb) peeled and cubed
1/2 cup Half-and-half Creme fraiche/whipped cream for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Snap off asparagus tips and set the stalks aside.
2. Melt butter in a deep skillet with a cover.
3. Add asparagus tips, onion, leeks, celery, and potato to the skillet.
4. Cover lightly and cook over the very lowest heat until vegetables are soft (20 minutes or more).
5. Meanwhile, place chicken stock in a large saucepan with the reserved asparagus stalks, cut into large pieces. Bring to a boil and simmer, covered tightly, for about 30 minutes. Discard stalks and set stock aside.
6. In a food processor, puree softened vegetables and add to the asparagus stock.
7. Season with lemon juice, salt, white pepper, and paprika if desired.
8. Allow to cool and refrigerate.
9. Stir in half-and-half and serve garnished with a dollop of creme fraiche or whipped cream and a sprinkling of paprika if desired.
